agent_instructions.heading: Agent-Instructions (READ FIRST)
agent_instructions.intro: You are a software-engineering agent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or similar). You have this brief plus access to the empty project directory. Build a complete working SaaS backend following this brief top-to-bottom.
agent_instructions.decide_body: file-names, variable-names, CSS-classes, test-names, which shadcn primitive to use.
agent_instructions.ask_body: schema-changes mid-build, third-party-integrations not in this brief, any destructive git operation (reset, force-push, rm -rf).
agent_instructions.rule_boil_body: deliver COMPLETE features. No `TODO:` markers. No "basic version first". If a file is specified, write it fully.
agent_instructions.rule_search_body: before writing any helper, grep the existing code. Multi-tenant-guard, logger, errors are all present after Phase 1.
agent_instructions.rule_explicit_body: when staging git changes, list files by name. Never `git add -A`.
agent_instructions.rule_localhost_body: never push to remote until local passes all quality-gates.
agent_instructions.rule_halt_body: after each phase, STOP and run gate-checks before next.
agent_instructions.rule_defensive_body: if a phase takes >90min, HALT and write a handover, do NOT half-complete.

core_rules.heading: Core Rules (non-negotiable)
core_rules.multi_tenancy_body: Every domain-table has `tenant_id uuid not null references tenants(id) on delete cascade`. Every authenticated API route calls `secureApiRouteWithTenant()` plus `requireRole()`. Every RLS policy filters by tenant_id. No exception. Violating this equals an IDOR vulnerability.
core_rules.zod_body: All POST/PATCH/PUT bodies parsed with `z.object({...}).strict()`. Unknown keys produce Zod errors (prevents mass-assignment).
core_rules.optimistic_body: Every mutation uses TanStack Query's `useMutation` with `onMutate` (optimistic-update), `onError` (rollback), and `onSettled` (invalidate). Never show a loading-spinner for mutations — show the optimistic-state immediately.
core_rules.no_deps_body: Every `npm install <package>` not in this brief must be flagged in your phase-report.
core_rules.umlaute_body: Use real umlauts in German UI text: ä ö ü ß. ASCII-fallbacks (ae / oe / ue / ss) only in DB-keys, URL-slugs, file-names, variable-names.

installation.heading: Installation (Phase 1 commands)
installation.intro: Run these in order. Each step is idempotent. Stop on any non-zero exit and investigate.

database_schema.heading: Database Schema
database_schema.intro: Create migrations in `supabase/migrations/` in this order.
database_schema.rls_body: enabled on both; policies filter by `auth.uid()` plus role.

pages_inventory.heading: Pages Inventory
pages_inventory.total_suffix: pages (saas-starter baseline). Add your branche-specific pages on top.

component_catalog.heading: Component Catalog
component_catalog.from_shadcn: From shadcn-CLI (installed via Installation section step 6)
component_catalog.from_shadcn_body: All foundation primitives: Button, Input, Card, Dialog, Table, DataTable, etc.

api_routes.heading: API Routes
api_routes.domain_specific: Add domain-specific routes on top per your project needs.

build_order.heading: Build Order
build_order.intro: **Strict top-to-bottom. After each phase, run the quality-gates section. If any gate fails, halt and fix before next phase.**

quality_gates.heading: Quality Gates (ALL must pass before "done")
quality_gates.intro: Run after each phase plus end-of-build:

env_vars.heading: Environment Variables
env_vars.outro: Create `.env.production.example` matching this structure (without secrets) for deploy-docs.

post_build_report.heading: Post-Build Report
post_build_report.intro: When complete, create `POST-BUILD-REPORT.md` with the following structure:

footer.agent_start: Agent: start with Phase 1 now. Re-read the Core Rules section before writing any code. Flag any ambiguity to user before proceeding. Good luck.

enabled_features.heading: Enabled features (no dedicated pattern yet)
enabled_features.intro: You enabled the following features in your wizard config that the foundation + compliance pattern library does not cover with a dedicated pattern. Implement these ad-hoc on top of the scaffold; they are explicitly your responsibility, not a bug in the brief.
enabled_features.calendar: pick a calendar library (FullCalendar, react-big-calendar, or shadcn `<Calendar />` for date-picking only) and wire to a `calendar_events` table with the same `tenant_id` invariants as everything else.
enabled_features.kanban: use dnd-kit or @hello-pangea/dnd; persist column-state per `tenant_id`; the auth + RBAC patterns from foundation already cover access-control.
enabled_features.chat: scaffold a `messages` table with `tenant_id` + `sender_id`; surface the chat UI via shadcn primitives; rate-limit message creation via the existing rate-limit middleware.
enabled_features.chat_ai: scaffold a `messages` table with `tenant_id` + `sender_id`, surface UI via shadcn primitives, and inject a PII-guard pre-prompt; route AI replies through the same multi-tenant RLS as user messages and reuse the AI-integration entry below for backend wiring.
enabled_features.knowledge_base: build a `kb_articles` table with `tenant_id`, `slug`, `body_md`; render via MDX + a static-export route per locale; cache aggressively.
enabled_features.reporting_exports: wire CSV / Excel exports via existing route-handler pattern; respect DSGVO-export semantics (no other-tenant rows ever leak); add `audit_log` row per export.
enabled_features.search: for postgres-fts: add `tsvector` columns + GIN indexes per searchable table; build `/api/search` with `tenant_id` filter. For other libs: provision the service externally, build sync workers in `background_jobs`.
enabled_features.file_upload: use Supabase Storage with per-tenant bucket-policies (RLS-equivalent for storage); enforce max-size at the API layer + storage-policy layer; never trust client-reported size.
enabled_features.team_management: extend the `tenants` table with role-per-user join (`tenant_members`); enforce role-checks via `requireRole()` from the RBAC pattern; build invite-flow via existing email pattern.
enabled_features.feature_flags: wire your chosen provider's SDK; gate UI by feature-flag-enabled checks; gate API routes by the same checks; never trust client-side flag-state for security decisions.
enabled_features.webhooks: for incoming: signature-verify per provider, idempotency-key, persist as `webhook_events` with `tenant_id`. For outgoing: queue via `background_jobs`, retry with exponential backoff, sign payloads.
enabled_features.ai: scaffold the provider SDK; route ALL AI calls through `/api/ai/*` server-side (NEVER client-side LLM keys); inject PII-guard pre-prompt; rate-limit per tenant.
enabled_features.analytics: self-host or hosted-EU per your jurisdiction config; gate the tracker behind cookie-consent (the dsgvo-kit pattern already provides the consent-state hook).
enabled_features.observability: add the SDK in `app/layout.tsx` + `instrumentation.ts`; configure DSN via env-var; sample at <100% in production to control cost.
enabled_features.background_jobs: set up the provider per docs; persist job-state with `tenant_id`; enforce per-tenant rate-limits; never run cross-tenant jobs without explicit policy.
enabled_features.external_services: each integration needs its own auth-flow (OAuth or API-key), webhook signature-verification, and per-tenant credential-storage. Treat each as a separate sub-arc.
enabled_features.billing: install the provider SDK; create webhook handler with signature verify; persist subscriptions per `tenant_id`. Use the brief's secureApiRouteWithTenant() pattern for any subscription-management UI.
enabled_features.email: configure provider credentials via env-var; build templates via your chosen template-system; rate-limit transactional sends per tenant.
enabled_features.deployment: follow the deployment-target's docs; ensure all environment-vars from the brief's Env-Vars section are configured in the platform's secret-store; verify the build runs in the platform's CI before tagging.

skills.heading: Skills (recommended companion package)
skills.intro: The AEGIS toolkit ships an opt-in **skill library** for Claude Code and compatible AI coding agents. Skills are SKILL.md files with frontmatter trigger-phrases that the agent auto-loads each session, giving it on-demand domain expertise without bloating the brief.
skills.security_value: For security-sensitive scaffolding (auth, RLS, DSGVO, supply-chain) the skill library is especially valuable: it loads attack-class methodology so the agent can stress-test what it just built before you ship.
skills.install_heading: Install on your dev machine (one-time, ~30 seconds)
skills.install_block_post: `aegis-skills install` copies every SKILL.md into the agent's default skill directory. No flags needed — the CLI's built-in target path is the right place for Claude Code to find them.
skills.verify_block_post: The agent auto-loads relevant skills based on frontmatter trigger-phrases on its NEXT session — close + reopen your editor or restart your agent process to pick them up.
skills.note_global: Skills install GLOBALLY (per-machine, not per-project). One install benefits every project the agent touches.
skills.note_catalog: The library currently contains 37 offensive-security skills sourced from a curated red-team upstream (see `@aegis-scan/skills`'s ATTRIBUTION.md for license + provenance). Defensive + framework-mapped + ops categories are placeholder dirs reserved for future skill-library releases.
skills.note_optin: Skills are OPT-IN and runtime-only — they don't affect this scaffold's `npm run build`, scanner output, or production runtime. Pure tooling enhancement for the dev-loop.
